note: Official expectation model only — live integration NOT executed.
authMethod: N/A (hosting integration). The ASP.NET Core Module v2 (ANCM) forwards requests from IIS to the in-process .NET runtime via web.config.
keyEndpoints: web.config aspNetCore handler section -> processPath, hostingModel=inprocess
GET / -> app served via in-process hosting
stdout / Event Log -> ANCM startup diagnostics

expectedRequestShape.requirement: ASP.NET Core Hosting Bundle installed; AppPool 'No Managed Code'.

expectedSuccessResponseShape.process: w3wp.exe hosts the app in-process; no separate dotnet.exe child for inprocess model

expectedFailureResponseShape.http: 500.30 ANCM In-Process Start Failure  OR  500.31 ANCM Failed to Find Native Dependencies  OR  502.5 ANCM Out-Of-Process Startup Failure
expectedFailureResponseShape.log: Event Viewer / stdout log shows the unhandled startup exception.

commonIntegrationFailure: HTTP 500.30/500.31 when the Hosting Bundle version is older than the app's target framework, or 502.5 when processPath/arguments are wrong; AppPool set to a managed CLR version instead of 'No Managed Code'.
diagnosticCommand: Get-WindowsFeature Web-Server; reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Updates\.NET"; Get-EventLog -LogName Application -Source 'IIS AspNetCore Module V2' -Newest 5
preventionRule: Install a Hosting Bundle >= the app's .NET target; set AppPool to No Managed Code; enable stdout logging only to diagnose then disable; match web.config processPath/arguments to the published dll.
